steps.title: Identify the grammatical role of the blank
steps.content: The phrase "a type of Chinese landscape painting ______ by the use of blue and green hues" is an appositive — a noun phrase that renames qinglü shanshui. An appositive must remain a noun phrase, so the blank cannot be a finite verb (which would create a second independent clause joined to the first by only a comma, i.e., a comma splice). The blank must be a non-finite past participle functioning as a modifier.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'has been characterized': finite present-perfect verb; turns the appositive into a second main clause, creating a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — 'will be characterized': finite future verb; same comma-splice problem. ✗
  • Choice C — 'characterized': non-finite past participle; correctly modifies the noun phrase without creating a second clause. ✓
  • Choice D — 'is characterized': finite present verb; creates a comma splice just like A and B. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
